behavior_summary: Provides the gameplay behavior for a "Recall" item: when the player uses the item it checks whether teleporting is allowed on the current map, and if allowed presents a menu of known teleporter destinations built from global "saw...Teleporter" flags.
Maps the player's textual choice into an internal destination code (with a conditional code choice for Carthax Lake depending on hydrosFree).
If the chosen destination code is non-zero it spawns a helper (U8P_RECALL::03C8) to play SFX, call the engine teleport routine (process teleportToEgg), and set the usedRecall global flag.
Also contains a simple look handler (U8P_RECALL::look) that displays the item's descriptive text via Item::bark.
notable_control_flow: Early-block: U8P_RECALL::use queries Npc::getMap() and early-exits (optionally playing/queuing a feedback SFX) when the player is on certain maps where recall is not permitted or should be treated specially.
Preparation: calls FREE::36E7 as a preparatory/cleanup step before building the UI list.
Menu construction: builds a selectable string list (slist) from multiple saw...Teleporter globals and always appends a "Nowhere" option.

Decision: presents slist with Item::ask, compares the returned string via strcmp against known labels to compute a numeric destination code (selected_code); Carthax Lake's mapped code depends on hydrosFree.
Action spawn: if selected_code != 0 then U8P_RECALL::use spawns U8P_RECALL::03C8 with parameters (destination code, pid) to perform the teleport; otherwise the function returns without spawning.
open_questions: Which concrete map IDs cause the early exit behavior and what in-game locations do they correspond to (the disassembly compared map IDs but their textual mapping is not present)?
What exactly does FREE::36E7 do (UI cleanup, memory free, or other preparatory action)?
What are the precise numeric destination codes and their mapping to engine map ids passed to process teleportToEgg (the script maps names to byte codes but the engine-level meanings are inferred)?
What is the exact semantics of process teleportToEgg's non-destination parameters (the second/third args observed in the spawn are not fully documented here)?

main_function.name: U8P_RECALL::use
main_function.purpose: Handle player U8P_RECALL::use of the Recall item: validate whether recall is allowed on the current map, present the player with a list of known teleport destinations (derived from saw...Teleporter flags), interpret the textual choice as an internal destination code (special-casing Carthax Lake depending on hydrosFree), and if a valid destination is chosen spawn the teleport helper to perform the teleport.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the item-U8P_RECALL::use event (called when a process/actor uses the Recall item).
Receives the caller process id (pid) as its execution context/parameter.

function_map.name: U8P_RECALL::use
function_map.role: Primary item-U8P_RECALL::use handler that implements the recall destination selection and spawn logic.

function_map.name: U8P_RECALL::look
function_map.role: Item look/examine handler; calls Item::bark with the static string "recall item" to describe the item to the player.

function_map.name: U8P_RECALL::03C8
function_map.role: Spawned helper that executes the teleport: plays SFX, calls process teleportToEgg with the chosen destination and pid, sets usedRecall = 1, and handles timing/suspension around the SFX.

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn the helper process that performs the actual teleport after a non-zero destination is chosen.

external_calls.purpose: Fetch the player's current map id to decide whether recall should be blocked or treated specially.
Check whether a particular SFX is already playing to avoid overlapping feedback sounds when recall is blocked.
Play or queue a feedback sound effect when recall is attempted on a blocked map or in other feedback moments.
Perform an unknown preparatory/cleanup step prior to building the destination list (likely UI or memory housekeeping).
Present the constructed destination string list (slist) to the player and return the chosen string pointer.
Compare the string returned by Item::ask against literal destination names to map the textual choice to an internal destination code.
Display the item's description text ("recall item") when the player looks at the item.
Play the teleport/activation sound effect when initiating the teleport sequence.
Invoke the engine teleport routine with player pid, the chosen destination code/map id, and an extra parameter to perform the actual teleport.

reads.name: sawCentralTenebraeTeleporter
reads.likely_effect: If true, adds "Central Tenebrae" to the recall destination menu.

reads.name: sawPlateauTeleporter
reads.likely_effect: If true, adds "Plateau" to the recall destination menu.

reads.name: sawUpperCatacombsTeleporter
reads.likely_effect: If true, adds "Upper Catacombs" to the recall destination menu.

reads.name: sawMountainKingTeleporter
reads.likely_effect: If true, adds "Hall of the Mountain King" to the recall destination menu.

reads.name: sawArgentrockIsleTeleporter
reads.likely_effect: If true, adds "Argentrock Isle" to the recall destination menu.

reads.name: sawCarthaxLakeTeleporter
reads.likely_effect: If true, adds "Carthax Lake" to the recall destination menu; mapped internal code depends on hydrosFree.

reads.name: sawDaemonsCragTeleporter
reads.likely_effect: If true, adds "Daemon's Crag" to the recall destination menu.

reads.name: hydrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Alters which internal destination code is used for the "Carthax Lake" menu entry (environment/quest state dependent).

writes.name: usedRecall
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 by U8P_RECALL::03C8 when a teleport is initiated to record that the recall item was used.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides the gameplay behavior for a "Recall" item: when the player uses the item it checks whether teleporting is allowed on the current map, and if allowed presents a menu of known teleporter destinations built from global "saw...Teleporter" flags.
